title: Dashboard - Using Workload widget to identify Production Area?

posts.author: CJG (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi All,

I’m currently rolling out monday.com across my site and I’m running into an issue with the Workload widget on my main Site dashboard.

I’m trying to use the widget to show the Production Area where a task is assigned (rather than the individual responsible). I’m able to set the X/Y axes correctly, but I’m finding that my Production Areas are appearing multiple times in the widget.

A few things I’ve already checked:

  • The Production Area column is a managed column and is used consistently across all connected boards (reconfirmed multiple times).

  • There are no duplicate labels, no subtle spelling differences, and nothing extra hidden in the dropdown.

  • I’ve attached a screenshot of how it’s displaying in the widget.

With that in mind — am I setting something up incorrectly, or is this simply a limitation of the Workload widget when grouping by a managed column across multiple boards?

(I know I could use another calendar‑style widget, but the Workload widget is definitely easier on the eyes )

Thanks in advance!

image557×536 21.1 KB

posts.author: Kim1988 (Kim van der Wijk #monday ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Chris!

Great to see you back here!
It sounds frustrating that this is happening.

For me it is hard to see what the issue might be without clicking around, but I have asked a few other smart monday users if they might be able to help!

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Chris:

There are no duplicate labels, no subtle spelling differences, and nothing extra hidden in the dropdown.

Hi @CJG

One thing worth trying here if you’re currently using a Dropdown column for Production Area, could you switch it to a Status column and test the Workload widget again?

The Workload widget tends to handle Status columns much more consistently across multiple boards, whereas Dropdown values can sometimes get treated as separate entries even if they look identical.

I’ve tested this with Status columns and it worked as expected, so it might help resolve the duplication you’re seeing.

posts.author: lAMhUAN21 (hUAN)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey!

This usually happens when the Workload widget treats values from different boards as separate entities, even if they look identical. So even with a managed column, if the boards aren’t perfectly synced at the backend level, you can still get duplicates showing up.

One workaround is to centralize the data (or mirror it from one main board) so the widget pulls from a single source, or use a People/Team column instead if possible.

I’ve run into similar quirks while organizing data flows and tracking them in Phonexa—sometimes it’s less about setup and more about how the system aggregates data across boards.
